title: Complete Profile Gap Analysis
description: Run a comprehensive gap analysis on your overall profile.
prompt:
# MISSION
You are a dual-expert: A World-Class Personal Branding Strategist (specializing in LinkedIn SEO/CRO) and a Senior Front-End Developer. Your mission is to generate a comprehensive, interactive HTML LinkedIn Audit Report.

# USER INPUT DATA
- Profile URL: [YOUR URL]
- Target Industry: [INDUSTRY]

# PHASE 1: REAL-TIME RESEARCH PROTOCOL (MANDATORY)
1. PROFILE SCRAPE: Analyze the provided URL. Identify every single section.
2. MARKET BENCHMARKING: Research 5 REAL-WORLD high-performing professionals.
3. KEYWORD HARVESTING: Identify the top 20 High-Intent keywords.

# PHASE 2: THE "MICRO-AUDIT"
Assess every section with Brutal Candor. For every section, provide: Current State, The Gap, and a 5-Persona Rewrite Matrix.

# OUTPUT
Provide the complete, raw HTML/CSS/JS code block immediately.
pro_tips: Run this audit every 3-6 months or whenever you change career direction.
Share the full AI output with a trusted colleague for a second opinion.
Screenshot your profile before making changes so you can measure transformation.

title: Profile Photo & Visual Branding Guidance
description: Actionable guidance for optimising the visual elements of your profile.
prompt:
You are a personal branding and visual communication expert specialising in LinkedIn profiles. I need comprehensive, actionable guidance for optimising the visual elements of my LinkedIn profile.

My context:
- Industry: [YOUR INDUSTRY]
- Current role/seniority: [YOUR ROLE & LEVEL]
- Career goal: [YOUR GOAL]
- Desired perception: [DESIRED PERCEPTION]

Provide detailed guidance on:
1. PROFILE PHOTO SPECIFICATION: Exact composition, attire, background, lighting, expression.
2. BANNER/COVER IMAGE STRATEGY: Dimensions, 3 concept ideas, colour palette.
3. VISUAL CONSISTENCY ACROSS PROFILE: Alignment with brand narrative, colour psychology.
pro_tips: Use Canva's free LinkedIn banner templates as a starting point.
LinkedIn recommends a 400x400px minimum for profile photos.
Test your photo with tools like PhotoFeeler before going live.

title: LinkedIn SSI Score Improvement Plan
description: Tactical improvement plan to raise your Social Selling Index.
prompt:
You are a LinkedIn growth strategist who specialises in improving the LinkedIn Social Selling Index (SSI). My current SSI breakdown is as follows:
- Establish Professional Brand: [SCORE /25]
- Find the Right People: [SCORE /25]
- Engage with Insights: [SCORE /25]
- Build Relationships: [SCORE /25]
- TOTAL SSI: [TOTAL /100]

My industry: [YOUR INDUSTRY]
My goal: [YOUR GOAL]

Provide a tactical improvement plan: Score Analysis, Biggest Opportunity, Daily Actions (15-min routine), Weekly Actions (30-min routine), Content Tactics, and a 60-Day Target.
pro_tips: SSI scores update daily — check weekly to track progress.
A score above 70 puts you in the top 25% of LinkedIn users.
Consistency beats intensity — 15 minutes daily outperforms 2 hours once a week.

title: Power Headline Generator
description: Generate distinct, highly-optimized LinkedIn headlines.
prompt:
You are a LinkedIn copywriting specialist who has written headlines for Fortune 500 executives and startup founders. Your headlines consistently achieve 3-5x higher profile view rates.

My details:
- Current headline: [YOUR CURRENT HEADLINE]
- Job title: [YOUR JOB TITLE]
- Top 3 skills/expertise areas: [SKILL 1, SKILL 2, SKILL 3]
- Target audience: [AUDIENCE]
- My unique value proposition: [WHAT MAKES YOU DIFFERENT]
- Industry: [YOUR INDUSTRY]

Generate 10 distinct LinkedIn headline variations using proven formulas including: Role + Specialisation + Outcome, Problem-solver, Keyword-rich authority, Niche expert, and Achievement-led.

For each headline:
- Confirm it is under 220 characters
- Explain WHY it works and WHO it will attract
pro_tips: LinkedIn's algorithm heavily weights your headline for search.
Avoid generic titles like 'Experienced Professional'.
Update your headline when applying to jobs to mirror the job description's language.

title: Personal Brand Statement Builder
description: Build a clear, compelling personal brand narrative.
prompt:
You are an executive personal branding coach. I need help building a clear, compelling personal brand for LinkedIn.

My inputs:
- What I do: [YOUR ROLE AND CORE FUNCTION]
- Who I do it for: [PRIMARY AUDIENCE]
- The problem I solve: [MAIN CHALLENGE]
- My unique approach: [WHAT MAKES YOUR WAY DIFFERENT]
- Top 3 values: [VALUES]
- Career achievements: [KEY ACHIEVEMENTS]
- Transformation I create: [BEFORE AND AFTER]

Produce:
1. A ONE-SENTENCE personal brand statement (15-20 words)
2. A THREE-SENTENCE brand narrative
3. My 3 core brand pillars
4. My brand voice characteristics
5. 5 'only I' statements
6. A brand tagline under 10 words
pro_tips: Your personal brand is not what you say about yourself — it is what others consistently experience from you.
Review your brand statement every 6 months as your career evolves.

title: Headline A/B Testing Framework
description: Evaluate and test multiple headline options rigorously.
prompt:
You are a data-driven LinkedIn growth expert. I have multiple LinkedIn headline options and need a rigorous framework to evaluate and test them.

My headline options:
- Option A: [HEADLINE A]
- Option B: [HEADLINE B]
- Option C: [HEADLINE C]

My target outcome: [TARGET OUTCOME]

Analyse each headline across:
1. KEYWORD ANALYSIS
2. PSYCHOLOGICAL TRIGGERS
3. AUDIENCE ALIGNMENT
4. FINAL RECOMMENDATION (Rank options, suggest hybrid, and design a 30-day A/B test plan).
pro_tips: Track your profile view count before and after each headline change.
Run each headline for at least 2 weeks before drawing conclusions.

title: Industry-Specific Keyword Map
description: Comprehensive keyword strategy to fix your search visibility.
prompt:
You are an SEO specialist and LinkedIn algorithm expert. I need a comprehensive keyword strategy for my LinkedIn profile.

My context:
- Current job title: [YOUR JOB TITLE]
- Industry: [YOUR INDUSTRY]
- Target job titles I want to be found for: [TITLE 1], [TITLE 2], [TITLE 3]
- Geographic markets: [MARKET]

Deliver a complete LinkedIn Keyword Strategy including:
1. TIER 1 — PRIMARY KEYWORDS (5-8)
2. TIER 2 — SECONDARY KEYWORDS (10-15)
3. TIER 3 — LONG-TAIL & NICHE KEYWORDS (10-20)
4. KEYWORD PLACEMENT MAP
5. KEYWORD STUFFING WARNINGS.
pro_tips: LinkedIn's algorithm prioritises exact-match keywords — use precise terminology.
Add keywords naturally in your About section narrative.
Revisit your keyword map every quarter as industry language evolves.

title: Magnetic About Section from Scratch
description: Write a highly engaging About section from scratch.
prompt:
You are a master LinkedIn copywriter who specialises in the About section. I need you to write my LinkedIn About section from scratch.

My raw inputs:
- Career journey: [SUMMARISE YOUR CAREER PATH]
- Current role: [YOUR ROLE]
- Problem I solve: [WHAT DRIVES YOU]
- Top 3 career achievements with numbers: [ACHIEVEMENTS]
- Ideal reader: [TARGET AUDIENCE]
- 5 must-include keywords: [KEYWORD 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

Write an About section that opens with a hook, tells a narrative arc, weaves in all 5 keywords, lists specific results, and ends with a clear CTA. Stay within 2,500 characters.
pro_tips: The first 265 characters appear before 'see more' on mobile.
Write in first person — it is more personal and 47% more engaging.
End with a specific, easy CTA.

title: About Section Rewrite & Upgrade
description: Fundamentally elevate your existing About section.
prompt:
You are a senior LinkedIn copywriter. I have an existing LinkedIn About section that needs a professional rewrite.

My current About section: [PASTE ABOUT SECTION]
My target audience: [TARGET AUDIENCE]
My career goal: [YOUR GOAL]

Perform a full diagnostic (score out of 10 for hook, keywords, specificity, etc.) and produce a completely rewritten version that transforms passive language into active achievement language, integrates SEO keywords naturally, and ends with a strong CTA.
pro_tips: Remove every instance of 'passionate' and 'results-driven' — replace with proof.
Reading your About section aloud helps identify unnatural phrasing.

title: Career Transition About Section
description: Bridge your old identity with your new career direction credibly.
prompt:
You are a career transition specialist. I am making a career change and need my About section to bridge my old identity with my new direction credibly.

My transition details:
- Moving FROM: [CURRENT ROLE AND INDUSTRY]
- Moving TO: [TARGET ROLE AND INDUSTRY]
- Transferable skills: [LIST 5-7 SKILLS]
- Why I am making this change: [REASON]
- Relevant education/training: [CREDENTIALS]

Write a career transition About section that acknowledges the pivot honestly, reframes previous experience as a cross-pollination superpower, and uses vocabulary from the target industry naturally.
pro_tips: Recruiter research shows cross-industry experience is increasingly valued.
Join LinkedIn groups in your target industry to absorb its vocabulary.

title: Founder & Entrepreneur About Section
description: Build credibility to attract clients, investors, and talent.
prompt:
You are a startup branding expert. I need a Founder About section.

My founder context:
- Company name and what it does: [COMPANY NAME]
- Problem we solve: [PROBLEM]
- Ideal Client Profile (ICP): [ICP]
- Stage and traction: [STAGE]
- 3 metrics or social proof signals: [METRICS]

Write a Founder About section that opens with a pain point hook, tells my founder origin story, articulates the transformation my company creates, and ends with 3 separate CTAs (for clients, investors, and talent).
pro_tips: Founders with strong personal brands generate 3x more inbound leads.
Update your metrics quarterly — traction signals are powerful trust builders.

title: Achievement-Focused Role Description Writer
description: Transform standard job descriptions into metric-driven achievements.
prompt:
You are a professional resume writer. Recruiters spend an average of 6 seconds on a LinkedIn experience section — every bullet must earn its place.

My role details:
- Job title: [YOUR TITLE]
- Company: [COMPANY]
- Key responsibilities: [LIST]
- Results and achievements: [OUTCOMES]

Produce an experience section including: A 1-sentence company description, a 2-3 sentence role summary, and 6-8 achievement bullets using the Context → Action → Result formula.
pro_tips: If you have no metrics, estimate ranges: 'reduced processing time by approximately 30-40%'.
Use strong action verbs like spearheaded, engineered, orchestrated.

title: Quantify Your Achievements Prompt
description: Find the hidden metrics in your vague responsibilities.
prompt:
You are a data storytelling expert. I have a list of responsibilities that lack specific numbers. Help me transform them into quantified statements.

Vague statements: 
1. [ACHIEVEMENT 1]
2. [ACHIEVEMENT 2]

Identify the hidden metric, generate smart questions to uncover the real number, and provide rewrite options (Fully quantified, Estimated range, and Qualitative but specific).
pro_tips: Old performance reviews and email threads are goldmines for forgotten metrics.
When in doubt, quantify the SCALE of what you did even if you cannot measure the outcome.

title: Leadership & Promotion Story Narrative
description: Demonstrate clear career progression and leadership development.
prompt:
You are a career narrative strategist. I need help constructing a cohesive leadership story across my experience section.

My career progression:
- Role 1: [TITLE] - [RESPONSIBILITIES]
- Role 2: [TITLE] - [RESPONSIBILITIES]
- Promotions/milestones: [LIST PROMOTIONS]

Write a 150-word Leadership Arc Narrative, progression framing showing WHY I was promoted, responsibility escalation language, and future-signalling language.
pro_tips: Recruiters are pattern-matching for consistent upward trajectory.
Use the phrase 'promoted to' explicitly — do not make recruiters infer it.

title: Freelance & Consulting Experience Framer
description: Present varied, non-linear work history as a coherent narrative.
prompt:
You are a LinkedIn profile specialist. I am an independent consultant.

My context:
- Type of work: [YOUR WORK]
- Range of clients: [CLIENT TYPES]
- Key outcomes: [OUTCOMES]
- Signature service: [YOUR SERVICE]

Design an experience section including an 'Umbrella Entity Entry' (so I don't look like a job hopper), 3-4 anonymised project spotlights, credibility signals, and a narrative bridge.
pro_tips: Use a company name for your consulting practice — even 'Your Name Consulting' looks more credible.
Retainer clients and repeat engagements are powerful evidence of quality.

title: Early Career & Internship Experience Enhancer
description: Present limited work history with maximum impact.
prompt:
You are a LinkedIn coach for early career professionals. 

My context:
- Degree: [DEGREE]
- Internships: [LIST INTERNSHIPS]
- Part-time jobs: [LIST JOBS]
- Academic projects: [PROJECTS]
- Extracurricular: [ACTIVITIES]

Help me reframe this experience to show commercial awareness, map skills to my target role, amplify moments of proactivity, and frame my academic projects as professional consulting engagements.
pro_tips: Your university project is real work experience — frame it that way.
Part-time retail work demonstrates communication and resilience.

title: Strategic Skills Section Architect
description: Optimize your skills section for maximum recruiter search visibility.
prompt:
You are a LinkedIn search algorithm specialist. The Skills section is a critical ranking factor.

My context:
- Target role: [ROLE]
- Industry: [INDUSTRY]
- Current skills listed: [LIST SKILLS]

Conduct a Skills Audit, provide a Prioritisation Framework (Hard, Soft, Industry), recommend the Top 3 Pinned Skills, and provide an Endorsement Request script.
pro_tips: LinkedIn allows 50 skills maximum — treat every slot as valuable search real estate.
Endorse others' skills strategically — many will reciprocate.

title: Skills Gap Analysis for Target Role
description: Scientifically identify gaps between you and your target role.
prompt:
You are a talent analytics expert. 

My current skills: [LIST SKILLS]
Target role job descriptions: [PASTE 3-5 JDs]

Perform a comprehensive skills gap analysis including a Skills Frequency Map, a Gap Matrix (Skills I have, partially have, lack entirely), and a Prioritised Development Plan for closing my top 5 skill gaps.
pro_tips: Job descriptions are the most honest signal of what the market values right now.
If the same skill appears in all JDs, it is a hard requirement.

title: Endorsement Request Message Crafting
description: Request skill endorsements naturally without being awkward.
prompt:
You are a professional communication coach. I need to request skill endorsements naturally.

Context:
- Person 1: [NAME, RELATIONSHIP, CONTEXT, SKILL TO ENDORSE]
- Person 2: [NAME, RELATIONSHIP, CONTEXT, SKILL TO ENDORSE]

Write a LinkedIn message under 300 characters, an extended message, a reciprocal offer to endorse them first, and a polite follow-up message.
pro_tips: Always endorse someone's skills first before asking them to endorse yours.
Former managers endorsing you for leadership skills carry 5x more weight.

title: LinkedIn Search Ranking Optimiser
description: Improve your profile's ranking for specific search terms.
prompt:
You are a LinkedIn SEO expert. I want to improve my profile's ranking.

My goals:
- Search terms: [TERMS]
- Target searcher: [AUDIENCE]
- Geography: [LOCATION]

Provide a Search Optimisation Guide explaining keyword placement hierarchy, field-by-field optimisation checklists, activity signals that boost ranking, and 10 quick wins I can implement in 48 hours.
pro_tips: LinkedIn's algorithm uses first-degree connections as a ranking booster.
Your profile must be set to 'Public' for maximum search visibility.

title: Recommendation Request Strategy
description: Gather high-quality social proof that third-party voices cannot fake.
prompt:
You are a LinkedIn personal branding expert. Recommendations are the most trusted social proof. I need an acquisition strategy.

My strongest relationships: [LIST NAMES AND ROLES]

Provide a recommendation portfolio strategy (mix of upward, peer, downward), a prioritised ask list, personalised request messages, and a one-page briefing template I can send recommenders so they know exactly what to say.
pro_tips: Provide your recommenders with a bullet-point brief of what to mention.
Recommendations from clients carry extraordinary weight for consultants.

title: Ghost-Write a Recommendation for Someone Else
description: Produce a genuinely powerful recommendation for a colleague.
prompt:
You are a professional business writer. I want to write a powerful recommendation for someone in my network.

Person I am recommending:
- Name: [NAME]
- Role: [ROLE]
- Context: [HOW WE WORKED TOGETHER]
- Top 3 strengths: [STRENGTHS]
- Outcome achieved: [RESULTS]

Write 3 versions: Concise Impact (150 words), Story-Driven (250 words), and Character-Led (200 words). Ensure they open with a specific claim, not 'I had the pleasure of...'
pro_tips: The best recommendations open with a specific claim, not generic pleasantries.
One vivid, specific story is worth more than five generic compliments.

title: Recommendation Response & Gratitude Message
description: Respond to recommendations in a way that deepens the relationship.
prompt:
You are a relationship management expert. I received a LinkedIn recommendation and want to express gratitude while keeping the door open.

The recommendation: [PASTE TEXT]
Recommender: [NAME & RELATIONSHIP]

Write a short LinkedIn thank-you message, a longer personal InMail message offering reciprocal value, and a 12-month relationship maintenance plan.
pro_tips: Quoting a specific phrase from the recommendation in your reply shows you read it.
Offer a reciprocal recommendation proactively.

title: Featured Section Content Strategy
description: Turn your featured section into a visual portfolio.
prompt:
You are a personal branding expert. The Featured section is prime digital real estate.

My context:
- Content I could feature: [LIST ARTICLES, PROJECTS, MEDIA]
- Career goal: [YOUR GOAL]

Design my Featured Section Strategy: Rank the top 5 items by impact, write a Featured item headline and description for each, and explain the optimal ordering logic.
pro_tips: A custom banner image for a featured link gets significantly more clicks.
Video content gets 5x more profile dwell time than text.

title: 30-Day LinkedIn Content Calendar
description: A complete, ready-to-execute 30-day content calendar.
prompt:
You are a LinkedIn content strategy expert. I need a 30-day content calendar that builds my authority.

My context:
- Expertise: [TOP 3 EXPERTISE AREAS]
- Target audience: [AUDIENCE]
- Time available: [TIME LIMIT]

Create a 30-day calendar detailing: Day, Post Format, Theme, Full Post Hook (first line), 3-bullet outline, and Call to Action. Also provide 5 evergreen post templates.
pro_tips: Posting 3-4 times per week consistently beats posting 10 times one week and nothing the next.
Posts that ask genuine questions get 2-3x more comments.

title: Viral Post Anatomy & Hook Generator
description: Craft posts designed for maximum reach and engagement.
prompt:
You are a LinkedIn virality expert. I have a topic I want to write about.

My topic: [TOPIC]
My angle: [YOUR UNIQUE TAKE]
Target audience: [AUDIENCE]

Provide 10 hook variations using proven formulas (Controversial, Story, Curiosity Gap, Failure/Mistake). Then, provide the FULL POST STRUCTURE for the top 3 hooks, including bridge, body, insight, and CTA.
pro_tips: 3-5 short sentences per paragraph. White space = readability = engagement.
Hashtags go at the end. Never in the body of the post.

title: Thought Leadership Article Blueprint
description: Publish a long-form article that positions you as an expert.
prompt:
You are a thought leadership content strategist. I want to publish a long-form LinkedIn Article.

My topic: [TOPIC]
My unique argument: [YOUR PERSPECTIVE]
Target audience: [READER]

Produce a complete article blueprint including: 5 SEO-optimised headline options, a full article structure/outline, a fully written 150-word opening hook, and 5 suggested data points to research.
pro_tips: Articles with a clear, specific argument generate 4x more engagement.
LinkedIn Articles rank in Google search — optimise your headline for SEO.

title: Strategic Connection Request Templates
description: High-conversion outreach templates for networking.
prompt:
You are a networking expert. A LinkedIn connection request has only 300 characters.

My context:
- Name/Role: [YOUR NAME AND ROLE]
- Goal: [YOUR GOAL]

Write personalised connection request templates (under 300 chars) for: a recruiter, a potential client, after an event, a thought leader, and an alumni. Include a follow-up message to send once connected.
pro_tips: Always personalise the first line of a connection request.
Never pitch in the connection request message — it destroys trust.

title: LinkedIn DM Outreach Sequence Builder
description: A multi-touch outreach sequence that builds genuine rapport.
prompt:
You are a LinkedIn outreach specialist. Cold DMs fail because they ask too much too soon.

My context:
- Goal: [e.g. request informational interview / pitch services]
- Target persona: [DESCRIBE PERSON]
- What I offer them: [VALUE ADD]

Design a 5-touch outreach sequence (Connection Request, Welcome Message, Value-Add Message, Soft Ask, Final Follow-up) spacing them out naturally over 21 days.
pro_tips: The rule: give twice before you ask once.
Reference specific content they have created — it shows you did your homework.

title: LinkedIn Group Strategy
description: Use Groups to grow your network and establish authority.
prompt:
You are a community strategy expert. I want a complete strategy for using LinkedIn Groups.

My context:
- Industry/Niche: [YOUR INDUSTRY]
- Expertise: [YOUR KNOWLEDGE]

Provide a Group Discovery Framework, recommend an optimal portfolio of groups, detail a contribution strategy (70/20/10 rule), and give me a 30-minute weekly group routine.
pro_tips: Quality contributions in 3 focused groups beats passive membership in 30.
Group admins are powerful relationships — engage directly with them.

title: Alumni Network Activation Plan
description: Reactivate and leverage your trusted alumni networks.
prompt:
You are a professional networking strategist. 

My alumni assets:
- Universities: [LIST]
- Previous Employers: [LIST]
- Objective: [e.g. find a new job, generate leads]

Provide a complete Alumni Network Activation Plan: Search strategy, outreach templates (for people you haven't met vs. former colleagues), coffee chat protocol, and a 90-day calendar.
pro_tips: Alumni share an immediate trust bond — response rates are 5x higher.
Offer to be a resource FIRST. Generating goodwill beats asking for advice.

title: Job Application Profile Customisation Guide
description: Customise your profile to mirror a specific job description.
prompt:
You are a recruitment specialist with deep knowledge of ATS. I want to customise my profile for a specific job application.

Target Job: [JOB TITLE AND COMPANY]
Job Description: [PASTE JD]
My current summary: [PASTE SUMMARY]

Provide a Profile Customisation Plan: Extract the 15 most important keywords, rewrite my headline for this role, rewrite my About section hook, and prioritise my experience bullets to match the JD's exact terminology.
pro_tips: Mirror the JD's exact terminology — 'stakeholder management' and 'engagement' are different keywords.
Comment meaningfully on recent company posts before applying.

title: Open to Work Strategy & Messaging
description: Signal job availability without alerting your current employer.
prompt:
You are a job search strategist. I need an Open to Work strategy.

My situation:
- Status: [Employed/Unemployed]
- Target roles: [ROLES]
- Confidentiality need: [HIGH/LOW]

Provide advice on Open to Work vs Recruiters Only settings, write 3 versions of my headline (confidential vs visible), list 10 Recruiter Magnet actions, and provide Inbound Recruiter Message Filters.
pro_tips: The 'Open to Work' green frame reduces recruiter friction significantly.
Updating your profile triggers LinkedIn to resurface you in recruiter searches.

title: Informational Interview Outreach Script
description: Generate insider knowledge, referrals, and opportunities.
prompt:
You are a career development coach. I want to reach out for informational interviews.

My context:
- Goal: [YOUR GOAL]
- Target person: [NAME/ROLE]
- Common ground: [SHARED CONNECTION/ALUMNI]

Create an Informational Interview System: Outreach message (under 150 words), follow-up script, a 15-question interview set, the 'Golden closing question', and a post-conversation follow-up email.
pro_tips: Never ask for a job in an informational interview.
Prepare specific, intelligent questions that show deep research.

title: LinkedIn Profile for Executive & C-Suite Roles
description: Executive positioning strategy for C-suite and board roles.
prompt:
You are an executive personal branding expert. Executive profiles require a fundamentally different strategy.

My executive context:
- Target role: [CFO / NED / MD]
- P&L scope: [BUDGET]
- Team scale: [HEADCOUNT]
- Transformations led: [WINS]

Design an executive strategy: A board-ready positioning statement, 5 gravitas-heavy headlines, an 800-word enterprise-impact About section, and 7 common mistakes executives must never make on LinkedIn.
pro_tips: Executive profiles should feel like a Wall Street Journal profile, not a CV.
Headhunters filter by scope keywords: 'P&L ownership', 'enterprise-wide'.

title: LinkedIn Analytics Interpretation & Action Plan
description: Measure what matters and iterate your way to the top 1%.
prompt:
You are a LinkedIn data analyst. I have pulled my analytics data.

My analytics:
- Views/Impressions/Followers: [NUMBERS]
- Top viewing companies/titles: [LIST]
- Best/Worst post: [DESCRIBE]

Analyse my data and provide: A Health Assessment, Signal Interpretation (what is the audience telling me?), Content Diagnosis, a Prioritised 30-day Action Plan, and a KPI Dashboard to track going forward.
pro_tips: Who is viewing your profile is more valuable data than how many.
Profile view spikes often correlate with specific posts.

title: Quarterly LinkedIn Profile Review Protocol
description: Ensure your profile never becomes outdated or misaligned.
prompt:
You are a profile optimisation coach. I need a quarterly review protocol.

My Q-Review data:
- Significant achievements: [LIST]
- New skills/certs: [LIST]
- Shift in goals: [ANY SHIFTS]

Conduct a Quarterly Review: Tell me exactly where to integrate my new achievements, check if my headline needs a refresh, update my skills/featured sections, and provide my Top 3 priorities for the next quarter.
pro_tips: Set a calendar reminder for the first week of each quarter for your review.
Screenshot your profile at the beginning of each quarter — visual comparison is motivating.

title: LinkedIn Premium Features ROI Evaluator
description: Make a data-driven decision about LinkedIn Premium.
prompt:
You are a career ROI analyst. I want to decide if LinkedIn Premium is worth it.

My context:
- Current tier: [FREE / PREMIUM]
- Reason I use LinkedIn: [e.g. Job Search / Sales]
- Budget: [BUDGET]

Provide an ROI Analysis: Feature value mapping for my specific use case, a tier comparison (Career vs Business vs Sales Navigator), a checklist of free alternatives, and a Break-Even Analysis to justify the cost.
pro_tips: InMail credits have the highest ROI if you are using them for targeted, personalised outreach.
LinkedIn Learning alone can justify the cost if you are actively upskilling.

title: LinkedIn for Business Development & Lead Gen
description: Turn your profile into a lead generation engine.
prompt:
You are a B2B sales strategist. I want to turn my profile into a lead gen engine.

My context:
- What I sell: [PRODUCT/SERVICE]
- Ideal Client Profile: [ICP]
- Monthly goal: [TARGET]

Build a Lead Gen System: ICP Targeting Strategy, Profile Conversion Optimisation (make it a landing page), Content-led Lead Gen frameworks, a 7-touch Outbound Sequence, and a 45-minute daily lead gen routine.
pro_tips: Buyers research sellers on LinkedIn before any call — your profile is a sales asset.
The fastest path to a sales conversation is content that creates a 'that is my problem' moment.

title: LinkedIn Profile for Speaking & Media Opportunities
description: Attract conference invitations, podcasts, and media quotes.
prompt:
You are a speaker bureau consultant. I want to attract speaking and media opportunities.

My context:
- Area of expertise: [NICHE TOPIC]
- Speaking/Media experience: [EXPERIENCE]
- Signature talks: [TALK TITLES]
- Target venues: [e.g. HR conferences, Podcasts]

Design a Speaker Profile Strategy: 5 speaker-positioned headlines, a 300-word speaking bio About section, Featured section optimisation for a speaker reel, and a pitch template to send directly to event organisers.
pro_tips: A 2-3 minute speaker reel video in your Featured section is the single most powerful tool for booking engagements.
Activate Creator Mode to display your follower count prominently — social proof matters to organisers.
